title: Essentialism
author: Greg McKeown
core_idea: Less but better. Essentialism is not about getting more things done — it's about getting the right things done. McKeown teaches a disciplined pursuit of less, helping you focus on what truly matters.
key_concepts: Almost everything is noise; very few things are essential.
If you don't prioritize your life, someone else will.
Trade-offs are real — saying yes to one thing means saying no to another.
The power of a graceful 'no' is underestimated.
Clarity of purpose enables better decision-making.
notable_quote: If you don't prioritize your life, someone else will.
practical_lessons: Create space to think and escape to focus.
Eliminate the trivial many to focus on the vital few.
Say no to good opportunities to make room for great ones.
Build routines that make execution effortless.
